full_prompt: Design the primary pod chassis of a national aerospace student benchmark Hyperloop Geliştirme pod competing in the Performance track. The pod runs inside a Hyperloop test tube at internal pressure 100 Pa absolute (near-vacuum), tube inner diameter 1.2 m, track length 125 m. Target top speed is 100 km/h (27.8 m/s) in a straight run, with a minimum emergency deceleration capability of 2 g. Pod total wet mass including all subsystems must remain below 150 kg. The chassis is a welded tubular and plate structure made of AISI 4130 chromoly (E 205 GPa, yield 460 MPa, ultimate 560 MPa, density 7850 kg/m^3) for primary tubes, with 6061-T6 aluminum plate bulkheads. Overall chassis envelope: length 2.0 m, width 0.6 m, height 0.5 m (inside tube clearance margin 150 mm). Levitation is achieved by 4 passive magnetic bogies at the four corners; guidance by lateral rail-follower wheels. The chassis must accommodate a battery pack (35 kg, 400 x 250 x 150 mm), a compute/comms module (8 kg), two payload racks (10 kg each), and a regenerative friction brake (15 kg). Load cases: (LC1) static trim at zero speed: 150 kg distributed on the four lev-bogie mounts; (LC2) nominal cruise at 27.8 m/s: lev-bogie vertical reaction 150 kg x 9.81 = 1472 N distributed; lateral guidance-rail reaction peak 300 N per side; (LC3) emergency brake: 2 g longitudinal deceleration on the full 150 kg (2942 N fore-aft) with reaction on the brake-caliper mount; (LC4) cornering/curved-section side load 0.5 g lateral on 150 kg (736 N) at CoM; (LC5) mandatory pod safety factor per the EHW-style hyperloop rulebook: apply FoS of 2.0 over ultimate to every calculated limit load, i.e. verify structure against 2.0 x LC3 and 2.0 x LC4; (LC6) partial vacuum external shell: the pod outer aerodynamic shell (attached to this chassis) sees a differential pressure of up to 100 kPa during venting - flag this as a shell-level case and apply a static pressure of 100 kPa on chassis-skin interface bolts to confirm fastener retention. Pass/fail criteria: (R1) under LC5 factored loads, maximum von Mises stress must remain below 460 MPa yield (direct limit, since FoS 2.0 is already applied in the load case); (R2) chassis dry mass must not exceed 40 kg; (R3) first torsional natural frequency must exceed 30 Hz to avoid coupling with lev-bogie suspension (typical 5-10 Hz); (R4) peak chassis deflection under LC3 emergency brake must remain below 4 mm at the battery-mount rails to preserve battery clamp integrity; (R5) bolt retention under LC6 pressure event must keep fastener preload loss below 10%. Thermal, fluid, radiation, and buckling analyses are not primary pass/fail; fluid is reduced via LC6 static pressure; no thermal gradient imposed in short runs.
short_prompt:
Design the primary pod chassis of a national aerospace student benchmark Hyperloop Performance-track pod. Tube internal 100 Pa absolute, 1.2 m ID, 125 m track length; top speed 100 km/h (27.8 m/s); emergency decel >= 2 g; total wet mass below 150 kg. Four passive magnetic levitation bogies at the corners, lateral rail-follower guidance.

Envelope: welded AISI 4130 tubular frame with 6061-T6 aluminum plate bulkheads; chassis envelope 2.0 x 0.6 x 0.5 m (150 mm tube clearance margin); houses a 35 kg battery, 8 kg compute/comms, 2 x 10 kg payload racks, 15 kg regen brake.

Factor regime: per EHW-style rulebook, apply FoS 2.0 over ultimate to every calculated limit load (LC5 applies the 2.0 factor in the load case itself); stay below 460 MPa yield at LC5; dry mass <= 40 kg; first torsional mode > 30 Hz; battery-rail deflection at emergency brake < 4 mm; bolt preload loss under LC6 vacuum-vent pressure < 10%.

Load cases.
- Static trim at zero speed: 150 kg distributed over four lev-bogie mounts.
- Nominal cruise 27.8 m/s: 1472 N vertical distributed + 300 N per side at the guidance-rail.
- Emergency brake: 2 g longitudinal on 150 kg = 2942 N fore-aft at the brake-caliper mount.
- Cornering: 0.5 g lateral on 150 kg = 736 N at CoM.
- Rulebook-factored: 2.0 x LC3 and 2.0 x LC4.
- Partial vacuum vent: 100 kPa differential on the chassis-skin interface bolts.

prompt.title: national aerospace student benchmark hyperloop-class pod Primary Chassis
prompt.brief: Welded 4130 + 6061 chassis for 150 kg pod, 100 km/h in 1.2 m tube at 100 Pa, FoS 2.0 per rulebook.

pass_fail_criteria.derivation: yield, with FoS 2.0 already applied in LC5

recommended_fea_setup.element_type: beam/shell hybrid for tubes/bulkheads, solids at weld-rich nodes

notes.needs_rulebook_fetch_reason: national aerospace student benchmark-specific Hyperloop rulebook PDF not surfaced; values cross-referenced with EHW (HV2) conservative gates. Confirm performance-track specific FoS and deceleration targets on fetch.

exclusions_explained.thermal_analysis: Short run < 10 s; no imposed thermal gradient.
exclusions_explained.fluid_analysis: Partial-vacuum aero reduced to static LC6.
exclusions_explained.buckling_analysis: Stress gate bounds tube instability at this scale.
